_meta.notes: Annual VT homeowner programs and key dates. Drives seasonal context across answer pages.

homestead_declaration.name: Vermont Homestead Declaration (HS-122)
homestead_declaration.what_it_is: A form filed annually with the Vermont Tax Department telling the state your house is your primary residence.
homestead_declaration.what_it_does: Without filing, your house is taxed at the higher non-homestead rate. Filing is also the gateway to the property tax credit (HI-144) for households under the income limit.

property_tax_credit.name: Vermont Property Tax Credit (HI-144)
property_tax_credit.what_it_is: Income-based credit reducing your education property tax.
property_tax_credit.what_it_does: Households under the 2026 income limit (~$115,000) may qualify for a credit. Determination letters mail in June-July.

button_up_vermont.name: Button Up Vermont
button_up_vermont.what_it_is: Annual statewide weatherization campaign launched by Efficiency Vermont every October 1.
button_up_vermont.what_it_does: Aggregates available state and utility rebates for weatherization and electrification, plus highlights any expiring federal credits.

december_tax_letter.name: Vermont Tax Commissioner's Annual Property Tax Projection Letter
december_tax_letter.what_it_is: The Tax Commissioner publishes the projected statewide property tax change for the upcoming fiscal year.
december_tax_letter.what_it_does: First public signal of how much tax bills will go up the following August. The Dec 2025 letter projected a 12% increase; the legislature is currently negotiating that down to ~7% for FY2027.

property_tax_bill.name: Vermont property tax bill
property_tax_bill.what_it_is: Annual bill from your town for property taxes (education + municipal).
property_tax_bill.what_it_does: FY2027 bills will reflect the legislature's negotiated rate change. Statewide average increase forecast at 7-12% over FY2026.

spir.name: Seller's Property Information Report (SPIR)
spir.what_it_is: Vermont's standard property disclosure form completed by sellers and provided to buyers.
spir.what_it_does: Covers structural components, utilities, water/septic, environmental hazards, and (since June 2024 under Act 181) flood history.

act_181_flood_disclosure.name: Act 181 Flood Disclosure
act_181_flood_disclosure.what_it_is: Vermont law (effective June 2024) requiring sellers to disclose flood zone status and prior flood damage.
act_181_flood_disclosure.what_it_does: Mandatory flood section in the SPIR. Buyers can sue for non-disclosure.

mud_season.name: Vermont mud season
mud_season.due_date_annual: Late March through late May (varies by elevation)
mud_season.what_it_is: Annual freeze-thaw cycle that turns dirt roads to mud and restricts heavy vehicle traffic.
mud_season.what_it_does: Many dirt roads close to heavy trucks. Outdoor projects (paving, landscaping, foundation work) are typically blocked until mud season ends.
